posts.title: Bending the Code: A Meal, a Kitchen Wizard, and Why Tacos La Villa Is the Future of Everything
posts.excerpt: When Aleja Bautista-Abad and her partner Luz opened Tacos La Villa La Verne, something was immediately different. I remember noticing it before I'd even eaten there. The energy was different. The space felt intentional.

posts.title: The Forge | The New Peace Treaty: Vibe Coders, Legacy Coders, and the AI That Needs Both
posts.excerpt:
Steve Jobs didn't change technology because he was his co-founder's equal as an engineer. He changed technology because he kept asking: why can't it do that?

Most vibe coders will fall off. The technical debt of their haunted-house code â code that looks functional until you turn on the lights and discover the plumbing isn't connected to anything â will collapse under its own weight. But some will change the world.

And here is the critical point: they're going to need those old-school traditionalists. To question them. To guide them. To take their concepts and make them structurally sound.

The legacy coder provides the skeleton. The vibe coder provides the soul.

Neither one, working alone with an AI, produces something worth trusting.

posts.title: The Source Code of Everything: The Beatles, the Forge, and the Technology That Changed the World
posts.excerpt: He hired a forty-piece orchestra and gave them an instruction that made every classically trained musician in the room slightly uncomfortable: start at your lowest note, end at your highest note, do not synchronize with each other, and make the journey in whatever way feels natural. The result was not music in any traditional sense. It was controlled chaos. It was analog randomization. It was what you get when you give a room full of highly trained musicians permission to abandon their training simultaneously.

posts.title: The Nuclear Reactor and the Bagel: Why AI Is Going to Burn the World Down Before It Figures Itself Out
posts.excerpt: The current era of artificial intelligence is being built on a foundation that every honest engineer admits is unsound. The architecture is a brute-force hack, not an elegant solution. The hardware is the wrong shape for the software. The energy costs are not a temporary growing pain ÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂ they are a structural consequence of eliminating the gates that made traditional computing efficient.
